page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external block storage attachment.

The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated mathematical and machine learning operations. In benchmark evaluations, the processor demonstrates strong multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 68,640.57 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 50,308.4. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression reach top-tier levels, registering 36.88 GB/sec and 15.37 GB/sec respectively.

Memory performance is characterized by a strong cached read-only small block bandwidth of 3,768.18 GB/sec, though large block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier at 176.07 GB/sec and 69.43 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are strong, with Redis SET operations reaching 83.69 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 80.18 GB/sec. For AI workloads, the instance achieves top-tier LLM inference speeds on small models (135M) with 3,667.57 tokens/sec for prompt processing, while maintaining strong performance on medium (7B) and large (70B) models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D48s_v7 offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require high CPU clock speeds and moderate memory capacity without the overhead of dedicated local NVMe drives. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale external storage independently. This hardware profile is suited for relational databases, web serving, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores running at 4.2 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio, and operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Its x86_64 architecture includes AVX-512 and Intel AMX extensions, contributing to top-tier single-threaded compression speeds and strong multi-core CPU performance. Benchmark data highlights strong database operations, with Redis SET performance at 83.69 million operations per second, and efficient CPU-based LLM inference. The D48s_v7 is suited for web serving, database workloads, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong database and multi-core CPU benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v7 is a general-purpose instance with 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and strong database and CPU benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and strong multi-core processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
192.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated mathematical and vector operations
Strong database performance with 83.69 million Redis SET operations per second
Decoupled storage architecture with no local drives, supporting Premium Storage
Suited for relational databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
